text: The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 was largely accidental. Fleming noticed that a mold called Penicillium notatum had contaminated one of his petri dishes and was killing the surrounding bacteria. He hadn't been looking for an antibiotic at all. It took another decade before Howard Florey and Ernst Boris Chain figured out how to mass-produce the drug, just in time for World War II.
label: human

text: The 2008 financial crisis exposed deep structural problems in the American banking system. Mortgage-backed securities, which had been rated triple-A by agencies that should have known better, turned out to be full of subprime loans that borrowers couldn't repay. When housing prices dropped, the whole thing fell apart like a house of cards. Lehman Brothers collapsed, and suddenly everyone was terrified.

text: My grandmother used to make this incredible chicken soup every Sunday. She never wrote the recipe down, just did it from memory, adjusting things as she went. I've tried to recreate it maybe a dozen times and it's never quite right. There's something about the way she chopped the vegetables, really rough and uneven, that I think actually mattered for the texture.

text: The thing about running a small business is that nobody tells you how much time you'll spend on stuff that has nothing to do with your actual product. text: Photosynthesis in C4 plants like maize and sugarcane differs from the more common C3 pathway in several important ways. C4 plants concentrate carbon dioxide around rubisco using an additional set of reactions in mesophyll cells, which reduces photorespiration and makes them more efficient in hot, dry conditions. This is why C4 crops dominate tropical agriculture.
label: human

text: We drove through Nevada last summer and I swear there's nothing out there for hundreds of miles. Just flat desert and the occasional gas station that looks like it hasn't been updated since 1987. But the sky at night was unbelievable. I'd never really seen the Milky Way before, not properly, and it honestly made me feel pretty small.
label: human

text: The French Revolution wasn't just one event but a series of upheavals spanning a decade. It started with genuine grievances about bread prices and aristocratic privilege, spiraled into the Terror under Robespierre, and eventually produced Napoleon. Each phase consumed its own leaders. The revolutionaries of 1789 would barely recognize what their movement became by 1799.

text: I tried learning guitar three separate times before it finally stuck. The first two times I quit after about a month because my fingers hurt too much and I couldn't even play a clean chord. text: The real problem with most diets isn't the food restrictions, it's the all-or-nothing mentality. You eat perfectly for two weeks, then have one bad meal, feel guilty, and give up entirely. I've found that just cooking at home more often and not keeping junk food in the house does way more than any structured diet plan ever did for me.
label: human

text: Tectonic plates move incredibly slowly, usually just a few centimeters per year. But over millions of years those small movements add up to continental drift, mountain formation, and ocean basin creation. The Himalayas are still growing because the Indian plate keeps pushing into the Eurasian plate. It's been doing this for about 50 million years.
label: human

text: The James Webb Space Telescope has already delivered results that challenge existing models of galaxy formation. Galaxies observed in the very early universe appear more massive and structured than theorists predicted. This doesn't break the Big Bang model, but it does suggest we're missing something about how quickly matter assembled into organized structures after the initial expansion.

text: Baseball statistics have gotten absurdly complicated. Back in the day you had batting average, home runs, and RBIs. text: Quantum computing represents a fundamental paradigm shift in computational capability.
